title: Docker Compose Service Port Mappings Validator with Incorrect Port Range

statement.markdown:
Write a function to validate Docker Compose service port mappings. The function should take a Compose service as input and return an array of port mappings with incorrect port ranges. A port range is considered incorrect if the container port is greater than the host port. For example, given the following Compose service:

service:
  containerPort: 8080
  hostPort: 8081

The function should return an empty array because the port range [8080, 8081] is correct. However, given the following Compose service:

service:
  containerPort: 8081
  hostPort: 8080

The function should return [[8081, 8080]] because the port range [8081, 8080] is incorrect.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a Compose service with containerPort and hostPort properties.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be an array of port mappings with incorrect port ranges. Each port mapping will be an array of two integers, the container port and the host port.
statement.notes: The input will always be a valid Compose service with containerPort and hostPort properties.
